title: Mid-run save — camp to preserve floor position
problem: The game has no way to pause a run mid-floor. Closing the app kills all exploration progress — fog of war, player position, stamina, and potion count reset. Players who need to stop mid-dungeon lose everything explored on that floor, which discourages longer sessions.

lineage.spawnReason: session continuity: players need a safe way to stop mid-floor without losing explored progress

analysis: The main SaveData already persists cross-run state (floor, tier, HP, XP, equipped). What it does not persist is in-session grid state: player position, fog-of-war per tile, which tiles have spawned encounters, and current stamina. These are stored in gridStore (Zustand, in-memory only). The mid-run save creates a separate AsyncStorage key (@d2game_midrun_v1) containing a MidRunData snapshot. Grid tile types are regenerated deterministically from seed, so only fog (FogState per tile) and encountered (boolean per tile) need to be stored. At 50×50 = 2500 tiles each, the snapshot is ~5KB. On resume, initFloor regenerates the base grid from seed, then fog/encountered are patched from the snapshot before playerPos and stamina are restored. Two save triggers: explicit CAMP button on the grid (user-initiated) and automatic AppState background listener (app backgrounded). Resume is available via a gold RESUME banner in ClassSelectScreen when a mid-run save exists for the selected class.
implementation: src/services/persistence.ts: MidRunData interface {absFloor, localFloor, seed, tier, classId, playerPos, fog: number[], encountered: boolean[], stamina, hpPotions, manaPotions, stPotions}. saveMidRun/loadMidRun/clearMidRun async functions + _cachedMidRun module variable + getCachedMidRun() for sync reads. loadMidRun() populates cache on bootstrap. src/store/gridStore.ts: restoreMidRunState(data) action — regenerates base grid via generateFloor(seed+localFloor), patches fog/encountered per tile, sets playerPos+stamina. src/store/gameStore.ts: resumeRun() action sets {runStarted:true, screen:'grid'}. clearMidRun() called in startRun() and endRun() to invalidate stale saves on new run or death. App.tsx: AppState.addEventListener('change') triggers saveMidRun snapshot on 'background' when runStarted && classId set. loadMidRun() called in bootstrap after loadGame(). src/screens/GridScreen.tsx: CAMP button in right panel → handleCamp() builds fog/encountered arrays, saves MidRunData, shows toast. src/screens/ClassSelectScreen.tsx: getCachedMidRun() checked on render; gold RESUME banner shown when midRun.classId === selected; handleResume() calls restoreMidRunState + restores potions from snapshot counts + calls resumeRun().

decisions.decision: Separate AsyncStorage key for mid-run (@d2game_midrun_v1) rather than embedding in SaveData
decisions.rationale: SaveData is version-locked and migration-gated. Mid-run saves are ephemeral checkpoints — stale data should be ignored or cleared without corrupting the main save. Separate key allows clearMidRun() to wipe the checkpoint without touching cross-run progression.
alternativesConsidered: Embed midRun field in SaveData — couples ephemeral and persistent data, complicates migration
Keep in-memory only — useless across app kills, which is the primary use case
decisions.recommendation: Separate key. clearMidRun() called on death and new-run start to prevent stale resume.

decisions.decision: Store fog and encountered as flat arrays (number[], boolean[]) not Grid[][]]
decisions.rationale: Tile types are regenerated deterministically from seed — storing them wastes space and risks divergence if monster data changes. Flat arrays are compact (2500 entries each), JSON-serializable, and indexed by y*GRID_W+x for O(1) patch on restore.
alternativesConsidered: Store full Grid[][] — 3× size, redundant tile types, migration risk
Run-length encoding — premature; 5KB is negligible for AsyncStorage
decisions.recommendation: Flat arrays. Patch loop runs in <1ms on device.

decisions.decision: Potion counts saved in MidRunData and restored on resume
decisions.rationale: Potions are bag items in inventoryStore (run-scoped, not persisted in SaveData). On fresh app start after kill, the bag is empty. Without saving potion counts, RESUME would silently give the player 0 potions — or refillPotions() would give them a full set, which is a meaningful exploit (die, close app, resume with full potions). Saving and restoring exact counts at camp time is both correct and cheat-resistant.
alternativesConsidered: Call refillPotions() on resume — grants free potions, breaks economy
Resume with 0 potions — punishes saving, discourages the feature
decisions.recommendation: Save hpPotions, manaPotions, stPotions counts. Restore by clearing existing potions and adding the saved count back via generateHpPotion() etc.

decisions.decision: AppState background auto-save in addition to explicit CAMP button
decisions.rationale: Players may be interrupted unexpectedly (phone call, notification pull, home button). Auto-save on background ensures the most recent state is always captured without requiring the player to remember to camp. CAMP button still exists for intentional saves with UI feedback (toast).
alternativesConsidered: CAMP button only — requires discipline, misses accidental closes
Auto-save every N steps — too frequent, unnecessary AsyncStorage writes
decisions.recommendation: Both: background auto-save (silent) + explicit CAMP (toasts confirmation). Same MidRunData format.

successCriteria: Player can tap CAMP on GridScreen → toast confirms save → close app → reopen → select same class → gold RESUME banner appears → tap RESUME → lands on grid at exact tile position with correct fog state, stamina, and potion count. Background close (home button) also auto-saves. Starting a new run or dying clears the mid-run save. TypeScript 0 errors.
knownLimitations: Mid-run save does not persist bag non-potion items — items picked up mid-floor are lost on app kill (they're only in inventory bag, not in SaveData). Players should visit town or stash before closing.
Only one mid-run save slot per class — starting a new run with the same class overwrites/clears the previous save
RESUME always restores to the camped position; no way to discard the save except by starting a new run or dying
bossDefeated resets to false on restore (conservative default — boss cannot be re-spawned since bossDefeated is persisted in SaveData from the main save path)
userGuide:
## Mid-Run Save (Camp)

You can save your progress mid-floor using the **CAMP** button on the dungeon grid screen. This records your exact position, explored tiles, stamina, and potions.

### To save manually
Tap **CAMP** in the control panel (right side, below TOWN). A toast confirms the save.

### Auto-save
The game automatically saves your position whenever you background the app (home button, incoming call, switching apps). No action required.

### To resume
1. Open the game — you'll see the class select screen
2. Select the class you were playing
3. A gold **⛺ RESUME SAVED RUN** banner appears above the DESCEND button
4. Tap it — you'll land exactly where you left off, with the same fog state, stamina, and potions

### When saves are cleared
- Tapping **DESCEND** (new run) clears the mid-run save for that class
- Dying clears the mid-run save
- RESUME itself does not clear the save — you can close and resume again

### Note on bag items
Non-potion items picked up mid-floor are **not** included in the mid-run save. If you pick up loot and close the app without going to town, those items may be lost. Use CAMP before closing, and stash important drops in town.
componentFile: src/services/persistence.ts (MidRunData, saveMidRun, loadMidRun, clearMidRun, getCachedMidRun), src/store/gridStore.ts (restoreMidRunState), src/store/gameStore.ts (resumeRun, clearMidRun calls in startRun+endRun), App.tsx (AppState background listener, bootstrap loadMidRun), src/screens/GridScreen.tsx (CAMP button, handleCamp), src/screens/ClassSelectScreen.tsx (RESUME banner, handleResume)

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: Initial full feature definition — camp button, AppState auto-save, ClassSelectScreen resume banner implemented

publicInterface.name: MidRunData
publicInterface.description: { absFloor, localFloor, seed, tier, classId, playerPos, fog: number[], encountered: boolean[], stamina, hpPotions, manaPotions, stPotions }

publicInterface.name: saveMidRun
publicInterface.description: Writes mid-run snapshot to AsyncStorage + updates in-memory cache

publicInterface.name: loadMidRun
publicInterface.description: Reads and caches mid-run snapshot from AsyncStorage. Called during bootstrap.

publicInterface.name: clearMidRun
publicInterface.description: Removes mid-run snapshot. Called on new run start and death.

publicInterface.name: getCachedMidRun
publicInterface.description: Synchronous read of in-memory cache. Use after bootstrap; always reflects most recent saveMidRun/clearMidRun.

publicInterface.name: restoreMidRunState
publicInterface.description: Regenerates base grid from seed, patches fog+encountered, restores playerPos and stamina

publicInterface.name: resumeRun
publicInterface.description: Sets runStarted=true, screen='grid'. Called after restoreMidRunState on RESUME.

codeSnippets.label: Mid-run snapshot save (GridScreen handleCamp)
Grid restore (gridStore restoreMidRunState)
